label: Minor nail injury without fracture, nail-bed laceration, or infection
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of minor nail injury/subungual bruising.

No open fracture, nail-bed laceration needing repair, crush injury emergency, infection, neurovascular compromise, diabetic-foot/PVD/neuropathy risk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Open fracture, fracture/dislocation concern, nail-bed laceration needing repair, severe crush injury, uncontrolled pain, infection, open wound with contamination, neurovascular compromise, or inability to use digit.
Diabetic foot risk, neuropathy, PVD, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed hand/podiatry plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, podiatry, or hand/orthopedic care if pain or nail healing is not improving.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports minor nail-injury framing and home protection/cleanliness concepts. Autonomous Phase 1901-2000 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed minor nail injury/subungual bruising without open fracture, nail-bed laceration needing repair, infection, neurovascular compromise, diabetic-foot/PVD/neuropathy risk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports minor nail-injury framing and home protection/cleanliness concepts. Autonomous Phase 1901-2000 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed minor nail injury/subungual bruising without open fracture, nail-bed laceration needing repair, infection, neurovascular compromise, diabetic-foot/PVD/neuropathy risk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of minor nail injury/subungual bruising
no open fracture, infection, or neurovascular red flags documented
